model_narrative: We plan an ammonia and fertilizer supply network. We decide which domestic plants to open for ammonia and which to open for fertilizer, how much ammonia to ship from each source into each plant, and how much fertilizer to ship from each origin out to each demand center. Sources include the domestic plants and an external import source. The objective is to minimize the total cost, which combines the fixed costs of building plants with the per-unit production and shipping costs of all the ammonia and fertilizer moved.

sets.name: i
sets.doc: all sources of ammonia; member 0 is an external import source and members 1 through 5 are the domestic plants

sets.name: id
sets.doc: the domestic plant sites; these are the sources that can be built and that produce ammonia and fertilizer, and they are exactly the non-import members of the full source set

sets.name: k
sets.doc: the demand centers that must receive fertilizer

sets.name: product
sets.doc: the two products handled in the network

sets.name: j
sets.doc: an alias of the full source set i, used as the origin index for fertilizer shipments

sets.name: jd
sets.doc: an alias of the domestic plant set id, used as the plant index for ammonia received and fertilizer produced

params.name: fc
params.doc: the fixed cost of erecting a plant, given per source and per product, in cost units

params.name: c
params.doc: the combined production and shipping cost per unit of ammonia moved from a source to a domestic plant, in cost units per unit

params.name: d
params.doc: the combined production and shipping cost per unit of fertilizer moved from an origin to a demand center, in cost units per unit

params.name: r
params.doc: the fertilizer demand required at each demand center, in units

params.name: bigm
params.doc: a large upper-bound constant equal to the total fertilizer demand summed over all demand centers, used to switch flows on or off with the build indicators

vars.name: x
vars.doc: the quantity of ammonia shipped from a source to a domestic plant, in units

vars.name: y
vars.doc: the quantity of fertilizer shipped from an origin to a demand center, in units

vars.name: u
vars.doc: tagged product shipment quantity by source, plant, and demand center, in units

vars.name: z
vars.doc: the build indicator for fertilizer at each domestic plant, one if the plant is opened for fertilizer and zero otherwise

vars.name: w
vars.doc: the build indicator for ammonia at each domestic plant, one if the plant is opened for ammonia and zero otherwise

vars.name: tc
vars.doc: the total cost accumulated across the whole network

constraints.name: x[i,
constraints.intent: Each domestic plant cannot send out more fertilizer than the ammonia it takes in. For every domestic plant, the total ammonia received at that plant from all sources must be at least the total fertilizer shipped out of that plant to all demand centers.

constraints.name: y[j,
constraints.intent: Every demand center must be fully supplied with fertilizer. For each demand center, the total fertilizer shipped into it from all origins must be at least its required demand.

constraints.name: bigm
constraints.intent: Ammonia can only be received at a domestic plant if that plant has been opened for ammonia. For each domestic plant, when the plant is not opened for ammonia no ammonia may be shipped into it, and when it is opened the incoming ammonia is capped by the large bounding constant. The total ammonia shipped into the plant from the domestic plants must stay within that gated limit.

constraints.name: bigm
constraints.intent: Fertilizer can only be produced and shipped from a domestic plant if that plant has been opened for fertilizer. For each domestic plant, when the plant is not opened for fertilizer no fertilizer may leave it, and when it is opened the outgoing fertilizer is capped by the large bounding constant. The total fertilizer shipped out of the plant to all demand centers must stay within that gated limit.

constraints.name: fc[id,
constraints.intent: The total cost accounts for both building plants and moving product. Add up the fixed building cost for every plant opened for ammonia and every plant opened for fertilizer, then add the per-unit production and shipping cost of all ammonia moved from sources into plants and of all fertilizer moved from origins out to demand centers. Set the total cost equal to that combined sum.

query: There's a proposal on the table to pair the two central sites: anytime the fertilizer line at site 2 is running, the line at site 3 should be brought online alongside it for regional redundancy. Tie site 3's fertilizer line to site 2's in the plan and tell me what the network cost comes out to.

query: We're looking at a supply-line pairing rule for the ammonia side: whenever the ammonia plant at site 2 is operating, the northern ammonia plant at site 1 should also be switched on to shorten the haul. Build that dependency into the model and walk me through what the total cost becomes.

query: The ministry is pushing a resilience rule — fertilizer production shouldn't sit in a single plant, so at least two fertilizer plants must be running. Layer that in and tell me the resulting cost and plant mix.
expected_pyomo: model.min_two_fert = Constraint(expr=sum(model.z[j] for j in model.jd) >= 2)

query: Suppose plant 2's intake gets throttled — it can take in at most 500 units of ammonia per period because of a pipeline limit. Cap the ammonia flowing into plant 2 at 500 and tell me how the plan and cost shift.

query: Site 2 is doing almost all the fertilizer work and the southern markets have no backstop at all. Couldn't we ease site 2's fertilizer output down below nine hundred units total and bring the southern site 5 line into service to cover the slack? Shouldn't a setup like that be on the table — what's the optimizer seeing that keeps it concentrated on site 2?
expected_pyomo:
model.cap_p2 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.y[2, k] for k in model.k) <= 900)
model.open_p5 = Constraint(expr=model.z[5] >= 1)

query: We're relying entirely on domestic production and the external market is sitting unused. Wouldn't it make sense to bring in at least a hundred and fifty units of fertilizer through the external source across the demand centers, rather than pushing everything through the domestic plants? What's the tradeoff that's keeping the plan from leaning on imports at all?
expected_pyomo: model.use_import = Constraint(expr=sum(model.y[0, k] for k in model.k) >= 150)
